title: Fix Object Toggle State Mismatch Bug
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want the Show Objects toggle to accurately reflect the panel state, so that I don't have to click twice to collapse it.
description: Fix a bug where the object browser toggle defaults to a disabled visual state while the object panel is expanded. This forces the user to click twice — once to sync the toggle, once to collapse. The toggle's visual state should always match the actual panel visibility.
acceptance_criteria: Toggle visual state matches the object panel visibility on page load
Single click toggles the panel in the expected direction
Toggle turns green when the panel is expanded
ux_changes: Fixed toggle state synchronization so the Show Objects toggle accurately reflects whether the object browser panel is expanded or collapsed.
implementation_notes: Toggle initialization logic was out of sync with the database-persisted panel state. Fixed by reading the stored preference on page load and setting the toggle accordingly.
comment_summary: Developer fixed the state mismatch and committed to better QA before submitting for review.
